Solution:

\text{Let } \alpha = \cos^{-1}\left(-\frac{\sqrt{17}}{17}\right).

\text{Based on the domain of } \cos^{-1} \text{ and the sign of the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } II.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = -1, \quad y = 4, \quad r = \sqrt{17}

\text{Evaluate the fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{4 \, \sqrt{17}}{17}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = -\frac{\sqrt{17}}{17}

\text{Apply the double-angle identity:}

\cos(2\alpha) = \cos(2\alpha) = \cos^2(\alpha) - \sin^2(\alpha)

\cos(2\alpha) = \left(-\frac{\sqrt{17}}{17}\right)^2 - \left(\frac{4 \, \sqrt{17}}{17}\right)^2

\cos(2\alpha) = -\frac{15}{17}

Solution:

\text{Let } \alpha = \csc^{-1}\left(-\frac{\sqrt{34}}{3}\right).

\text{Based on the domain of } \csc^{-1} \text{ and the sign of the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } IV.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 5, \quad y = -3, \quad r = \sqrt{34}

\text{Evaluate the fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = -\frac{3 \, \sqrt{34}}{34}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{5 \, \sqrt{34}}{34}

\text{Apply the double-angle identity:}

\sin(2\alpha) = \sin(2\alpha) = 2\sin(\alpha)\cos(\alpha)

\sin(2\alpha) = 2\left(-\frac{3 \, \sqrt{34}}{34}\right)\left(\frac{5 \, \sqrt{34}}{34}\right)

\sin(2\alpha) = -\frac{15}{17}

\cos(2\alpha) = \left(-\frac{2 \, \sqrt{29}}{29}\right)^2 - \left(\frac{5 \, \sqrt{29}}{29}\right)^2

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 4, \quad y = 1, \quad r = \sqrt{17}

\text{Evaluate the fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{\sqrt{17}}{17}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{4 \, \sqrt{17}}{17}

\text{Apply the double-angle identity:}

\cos(2\alpha) = \cos(2\alpha) = \cos^2(\alpha) - \sin^2(\alpha)

\cos(2\alpha) = \left(\frac{4 \, \sqrt{17}}{17}\right)^2 - \left(\frac{\sqrt{17}}{17}\right)^2

\cos(2\alpha) = \frac{15}{17}

\text{Evaluate the fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{2 \, \sqrt{13}}{13}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = -\frac{3 \, \sqrt{13}}{13}

\text{Apply the double-angle identity:}

\cos(2\alpha) = \cos(2\alpha) = \cos^2(\alpha) - \sin^2(\alpha)

\cos(2\alpha) = \left(-\frac{3 \, \sqrt{13}}{13}\right)^2 - \left(\frac{2 \, \sqrt{13}}{13}\right)^2

\cos(2\alpha) = \frac{5}{13}

\text{Evaluate the fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = -\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}

\cos(2\alpha) = \cos(2\alpha) = \cos^2(\alpha) - \sin^2(\alpha)

\cos(2\alpha) = \left(\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}\right)^2 - \left(-\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}\right)^2

\cos(2\alpha) = 0

\text{Evaluate the fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{3}{5}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{4}{5}

\sin(2\alpha) = \sin(2\alpha) = 2\sin(\alpha)\cos(\alpha)

\sin(2\alpha) = 2\left(\frac{3}{5}\right)\left(\frac{4}{5}\right)

\sin(2\alpha) = \frac{24}{25}

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 4, \quad y = 1, \quad r = \sqrt{17}

\text{Evaluate the fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{\sqrt{17}}{17}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{4 \, \sqrt{17}}{17}

\text{Apply the double-angle identity:}

\cos(2\alpha) = \cos(2\alpha) = \cos^2(\alpha) - \sin^2(\alpha)

\cos(2\alpha) = \left(\frac{4 \, \sqrt{17}}{17}\right)^2 - \left(\frac{\sqrt{17}}{17}\right)^2

\cos(2\alpha) = \frac{15}{17}

\text{Based on the domain of } \cos^{-1} \text{ and the sign of the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } II.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = -3, \quad y = 5, \quad r = \sqrt{34}

\text{Evaluate the fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{5 \, \sqrt{34}}{34}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = -\frac{3 \, \sqrt{34}}{34}

\text{Apply the double-angle identity:}

\sin(2\alpha) = \sin(2\alpha) = 2\sin(\alpha)\cos(\alpha)

\sin(2\alpha) = 2\left(\frac{5 \, \sqrt{34}}{34}\right)\left(-\frac{3 \, \sqrt{34}}{34}\right)

\sin(2\alpha) = -\frac{15}{17}

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 1, \quad y = -3, \quad r = \sqrt{10}

\text{Evaluate the fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = -\frac{3 \, \sqrt{10}}{10}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{\sqrt{10}}{10}

\text{Apply the double-angle identity:}

\cos(2\alpha) = \cos(2\alpha) = \cos^2(\alpha) - \sin^2(\alpha)

\cos(2\alpha) = \left(\frac{\sqrt{10}}{10}\right)^2 - \left(-\frac{3 \, \sqrt{10}}{10}\right)^2

\cos(2\alpha) = -\frac{4}{5}

\text{Based on the domain of } \sec^{-1} \text{ and the sign of the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } I.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 4, \quad y = 1, \quad r = \sqrt{17}

\text{Evaluate the fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{\sqrt{17}}{17}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{4 \, \sqrt{17}}{17}

\text{Apply the double-angle identity:}

\cos(2\alpha) = \cos(2\alpha) = \cos^2(\alpha) - \sin^2(\alpha)

\cos(2\alpha) = \left(\frac{4 \, \sqrt{17}}{17}\right)^2 - \left(\frac{\sqrt{17}}{17}\right)^2

\cos(2\alpha) = \frac{15}{17}

\text{Apply the double-angle identity:}

\cos(2\alpha) = \cos(2\alpha) = \cos^2(\alpha) - \sin^2(\alpha)

\cos(2\alpha) = \left(-\frac{5 \, \sqrt{29}}{29}\right)^2 - \left(\frac{2 \, \sqrt{29}}{29}\right)^2

\cos(2\alpha) = \frac{21}{29}

Solution:

\text{Let } \alpha = \tan^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right).

\text{Based on the domain of } \tan^{-1} \text{ and the sign of the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } I.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 4, \quad y = 2, \quad r = 2 \, \sqrt{5}

\text{Evaluate the fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{\sqrt{5}}{5}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{2 \, \sqrt{5}}{5}

\text{Apply the double-angle identity:}

\cos(2\alpha) = \cos(2\alpha) = \cos^2(\alpha) - \sin^2(\alpha)

\cos(2\alpha) = \left(\frac{2 \, \sqrt{5}}{5}\right)^2 - \left(\frac{\sqrt{5}}{5}\right)^2

\cos(2\alpha) = \frac{3}{5}

Solution:

\text{Let } \alpha = \csc^{-1}\left(-\sqrt{17}\right).

\text{Based on the domain of } \csc^{-1} \text{ and the sign of the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } IV.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 4, \quad y = -1, \quad r = \sqrt{17}

\text{Evaluate the fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = -\frac{\sqrt{17}}{17}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{4 \, \sqrt{17}}{17}

\text{Apply the double-angle identity:}

\sin(2\alpha) = \sin(2\alpha) = 2\sin(\alpha)\cos(\alpha)

\sin(2\alpha) = 2\left(-\frac{\sqrt{17}}{17}\right)\left(\frac{4 \, \sqrt{17}}{17}\right)

\sin(2\alpha) = -\frac{8}{17}

Solution:

\text{Let } \alpha = \cos^{-1}\left(-\frac{\sqrt{17}}{17}\right).

\text{Based on the domain of } \cos^{-1} \text{ and the sign of the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } II.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = -1, \quad y = 4, \quad r = \sqrt{17}

\text{Evaluate the fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{4 \, \sqrt{17}}{17}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = -\frac{\sqrt{17}}{17}

\text{Apply the double-angle identity:}

\sin(2\alpha) = \sin(2\alpha) = 2\sin(\alpha)\cos(\alpha)

\sin(2\alpha) = 2\left(\frac{4 \, \sqrt{17}}{17}\right)\left(-\frac{\sqrt{17}}{17}\right)

\sin(2\alpha) = -\frac{8}{17}

Solution:

\text{Let } \alpha = \tan^{-1}\left(-\frac{1}{3}\right).

\text{Based on the domain of } \tan^{-1} \text{ and the sign of the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } IV.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 3, \quad y = -1, \quad r = \sqrt{10}

\text{Evaluate the fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = -\frac{\sqrt{10}}{10}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{3 \, \sqrt{10}}{10}

\text{Apply the double-angle identity:}

\sin(2\alpha) = \sin(2\alpha) = 2\sin(\alpha)\cos(\alpha)

\sin(2\alpha) = 2\left(-\frac{\sqrt{10}}{10}\right)\left(\frac{3 \, \sqrt{10}}{10}\right)

\sin(2\alpha) = -\frac{3}{5}
